steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The container starts at 21 mL. One 0.06 mL drop falls every 6 seconds, so the rate is $\frac{0.06}{6}=0.01$ mL per second. The question asks which equation models $v$ (total volume) after $t$ seconds.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $v=6t$: ignores the starting 21 mL and uses the wrong rate. ✗
  • Choice B — $v=0.36t+21$: rate 0.36 mL/s would mean 0.36×6=2.16 mL per drop, far too fast. ✗
  • Choice C — $v=0.01t+21$: starts at 21 mL and adds 0.01 mL each second, matching the calculated rate. ✓
  • Choice D — $v=0.06t+21$: uses 0.06 mL/s instead of 0.06 mL per 6 s — off by a factor of 6. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
